<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Checking the schema to determine if an index relationship benefits a pair of notes.

Index is designed for hub pages that gather many notes together, not for pairs. Only three hub pages currently use this relationship. For two individual notes, pairwise relationships like extends, contradicts, or implements would be more appropriate.</details></think>

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

**No — not for a pair of atomic notes.** In your garden, `index` means one note **gathers many others**, not a link between two peers.

From [[Note Relationships]]:

| Type | Question |
|------|----------|
| **index** | What gathers many notes? |

Your flashcard frames it the same way: a hub page gathering ~20 related notes → mark **that page** as `index`. The live examples are all hubs: [[Getting Started]], [[Eternal Principles]], [[Maps of Content]].

For **two notes**, the useful types are:

- **extends** — A builds on B  
- **contradicts** — A pushes against B (with a `when` in Reason)  
- **implements** — A carries B out in practice  
- **alternative** — A and B solve the same job different ways  

If note A only “points at” note B, that’s **extends** (or **alternative** if they’re interchangeable tools). `index` would mislabel the relationship — it implies A is a table-of-contents for a cluster, not a single link to one neighbor.

**When index does help:** on the **hub note itself**, one row like `| index | — | Index for gospel notes in this garden |` tells the linter and graph “this page is the MOC, not a claim.” That’s why your spec says index/MOC pages use an **index** row **instead of** the usual extends+contradicts minimum — not as a pairwise link between two atomic notes.

**Rule of thumb:** pair of notes → extends/contradicts/implements/alternative. One note listing many → `index` on the hub only.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Preparing comprehensive answers to all three questions, starting with useful pair types.

Other vocabulary types like supports/refutes or enables/requires exist but the current four pair types already cover most cases well. "Pairs with" was demoted to informal extends and pipeline order is prose rather than a type. Removing index and making it a flag is valid because index is a page-level property, not a pairwise link.</details></think>

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

**Other pair types?** Your four cover almost everything for atomic notes:

| Type | Pair question |
|------|----------------|
| **extends** | What does this build on? |
| **contradicts** | What tradeoff does this push against? |
| **implements** | What does this make real? |
| **alternative** | What else could do this job? |

Candidates people add elsewhere — **requires**, **enables**, **example of**, **refines**, **inspired by** — usually collapse into one of yours:

- A needs B first → **extends** (with reason: "assumes X")
- A is a case of B → **extends**
- A and B are peers, pick one → **alternative**
- A operationalizes B → **implements**

I'd skip a fifth pair type unless you keep hitting the same gap. "Pairs with" is already informal **extends** in your docs. Pipeline order (capture → distill → express) stays prose — not a link type.

---

**Index as a flag instead of a relationship type?** Yes — that's the cleaner model.

`index` isn't pairwise. It describes the **page**: "this note gathers many others." Right now that's a table row on 3 hubs ([[Getting Started]], [[Eternal Principles]], [[Maps of Content]]), and the linter reads it from body text via `relationship-types.html`.

Utility already works this way:

```yaml
note_kind: meta   # Graph, Issues, Review, Flashcards, etc.
```

Handled by `is-utility-page.html` — excluded from graph nodes, random pool, card sources; no `## Note Relationships` table; URL links only; `npm run lint:utility-links`.

Index could mirror that:

```yaml
note_kind: index   # or note_kind: moc
```

Then:

- Hub pages skip the extends/contradicts minimum (already how `$rels.index` works in `collect-note-issues.html`)
- Graph can treat hubs via frontmatter instead of parsing `| index |` rows
- The table becomes **pair-only**: extends, contradicts, implements, alternative
- Reason column stays meaningful on every row — no more `| index | — | Index for … |` filler

`getting-started` is a slight edge case: both site index and onboarding note. You could use `note_kind: index` plus normal extends/contradicts if you want typed links there too.

---

**Does utility have a flag?** Yes — **`note_kind: meta`**, with layout name as fallback (`graph`, `review`, `queue`, etc.) in `is-utility-page.html`.

| Kind | What it is | Relationships table | Wikilink from content notes? |
|------|------------|---------------------|------------------------------|
| `note` (default) | Atomic garden claim | Required (extends + contradicts) | Yes |
| `meta` | Tool surface | Skipped | No — URL only |
| `index` (proposed) | MOC / hub | Skipped or optional | Yes — hubs are ideas, not tools |

---

**Recommendation:** Drop `index` from the five relationship types. Promote it to `note_kind: index` (or `moc`). Keep the table for **pairs only**. You already have the pattern with `meta`; index is the same idea — page role, not link semantics.

Want me to implement that migration (frontmatter on the 3 hub notes, update linter/graph/templates, remove index rows from tables)?

---

_**User**_

yes. sort the types by type. review all notes. all possible wikilinks for all 4 types. even consider if it does not have a not yet. 

---

The concrete implementation steps are now clear. The `index` flag will be applied to getting-started, eternal-principles, and maps-of-content. <details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd /Users/jonathanrapusas/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && python3 << 'PY'
import re, os, json
from pathlib import Path

NOTES = Path("content/english/notes")
TYPE_ORDER = {"alternative": 0, "contradicts": 1, "extends": 2, "implements": 3}
row_re = re.compile(r"^\|\s*(alternative|contradicts|extends|implements|index)\s*\|\s*(\[\[[^\]]+\]\]|—)\s*\|\s*(.+?)\s*\|$")

stats = {"notes": 0, "missing_alt": 0, "missing_impl": 0, "has_index_row": 0, "unsorted": 0, "meta": 0, "index_kind": 0}
by_note = []

for f in sorted(NOTES.glob("*.md")):
    if f.name == "_index.md":
        continue
    text = f.read_text()
    fm, _, body = text.partition("---\n", 2)
    if body.startswith("---"):
        parts = text.split("---", 2)
        body = parts[2] if len(parts) > 2 else ""
    m = re.search(r'note_kind:\s*"(\w+)"', text)
    kind = m.group(1) if m else "note"
    if kind == "meta":
        stats["meta"] += 1
        continue
    if "## Note Relationships" not in body:
        by_note.append((f.stem, kind, "no_table", [], set()))
        continue
    stats["notes"] += 1
    section = body.split("## Note Relationships", 1)[1].split("## ", 1)[0]
    rows = []
    types = set()
    for line in section.splitlines():
        rm = row_re.match(line.strip())
        if rm:
            t, link, reason = rm.groups()
            rows.append((t, link, reason))
            types.add(t)
            if t == "index":
                stats["has_index_row"] += 1
    order = [TYPE_ORDER.get(r[0], 99) for r in rows]
    if order != sorted(order):
        stats["unsorted"] += 1
    if "alternative" not in types:
        stats["missing_alt"] += 1
    if "implements" not in types:
        stats["missing_impl"] += 1
    by_note.append((f.stem, kind, "ok", rows, types))

print(json.dumps(stats, indent=2))
print("index rows:", [n for n,k,s,r,t in by_note if "index" in t])
print("no table:", [n for n,k,s,r,t in by_note if s=="no_table"])
PY</summary>

```bash
cd /Users/jonathanrapusas/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && python3 << 'PY'
import re, os, json
from pathlib import Path

NOTES = Path("content/english/notes")
TYPE_ORDER = {"alternative": 0, "contradicts": 1, "extends": 2, "implements": 3}
row_re = re.compile(r"^\|\s*(alternative|contradicts|extends|implements|index)\s*\|\s*(\[\[[^\]]+\]\]|—)\s*\|\s*(.+?)\s*\|$")

stats = {"notes": 0, "missing_alt": 0, "missing_impl": 0, "has_index_row": 0, "unsorted": 0, "meta": 0, "index_kind": 0}
by_note = []

for f in sorted(NOTES.glob("*.md")):
    if f.name == "_index.md":
        continue
    text = f.read_text()
    fm, _, body = text.partition("---\n", 2)
    if body.startswith("---"):
        parts = text.split("---", 2)
        body = parts[2] if len(parts) > 2 else ""
    m = re.search(r'note_kind:\s*"(\w+)"', text)
    kind = m.group(1) if m else "note"
    if kind == "meta":
        stats["meta"] += 1
        continue
    if "## Note Relationships" not in body:
        by_note.append((f.stem, kind, "no_table", [], set()))
        continue
    stats["notes"] += 1
    section = body.split("## Note Relationships", 1)[1].split("## ", 1)[0]
    rows = []
    types = set()
    for line in section.splitlines():
        rm = row_re.match(line.strip())
        if rm:
            t, link, reason = rm.groups()
            rows.append((t, link, reason))
            types.add(t)
            if t == "index":
                stats["has_index_row"] += 1
    order = [TYPE_ORDER.get(r[0], 99) for r in rows]
    if order != sorted(order):
        stats["unsorted"] += 1
    if "alternative" not in types:
        stats["missing_alt"] += 1
    if "implements" not in types:
        stats["missing_impl"] += 1
    by_note.append((f.stem, kind, "ok", rows, types))

print(json.dumps(stats, indent=2))
print("index rows:", [n for n,k,s,r,t in by_note if "index" in t])
print("no table:", [n for n,k,s,r,t in by_note if s=="no_table"])
PY
```

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd /Users/jonathanrapusas/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && python3 << 'PY'
import re
from pathlib import Path

NOTES = Path("content/english/notes")
row_re = re.compile(r"^\|\s*(alternative|contradicts|extends|implements|index)\s*\|\s*(\[\[[^\]]+\]\]|—)\s*\|\s*(.+?)\s*\|$")

for f in sorted(NOTES.glob("*.md")):
    if f.name.startswith("_"): continue
    text = f.read_text()
    if 'note_kind: "meta"' in text: continue
    if "## Note Relationships" not in text:
        print(f"NO TABLE: {f.stem}")
        continue
    body = text.split("---", 2)[-1]
    section = body.split("## Note Relationships", 1)[1].split("\n## ", 1)[0]
    rows = [row_re.match(l.strip()).groups() for l in section.splitlines() if row_re.match(l.strip())]
    types = {r[0] for r in rows}
    missing = [t for t in ("alternative","contradicts","extends","implements") if t not in types]
    if missing:
        print(f"{f.stem}: missing {missing}")
PY</summary>

```bash
cd /Users/jonathanrapusas/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && python3 << 'PY'
import re
from pathlib import Path

NOTES = Path("content/english/notes")
row_re = re.compile(r"^\|\s*(alternative|contradicts|extends|implements|index)\s*\|\s*(\[\[[^\]]+\]\]|—)\s*\|\s*(.+?)\s*\|$")

for f in sorted(NOTES.glob("*.md")):
    if f.name.startswith("_"): continue
    text = f.read_text()
    if 'note_kind: "meta"' in text: continue
    if "## Note Relationships" not in text:
        print(f"NO TABLE: {f.stem}")
        continue
    body = text.split("---", 2)[-1]
    section = body.split("## Note Relationships", 1)[1].split("\n## ", 1)[0]
    rows = [row_re.match(l.strip()).groups() for l in section.splitlines() if row_re.match(l.strip())]
    types = {r[0] for r in rows}
    missing = [t for t in ("alternative","contradicts","extends","implements") if t not in types]
    if missing:
        print(f"{f.stem}: missing {missing}")
PY
```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Writing the comprehensive sync script directly in the file rather than using a Task agent.

Defining the four relationship types in order: alternative, contradicts, extends, and implements. The script will parse frontmatter and body, tag index notes, merge additions keyed by type and link, sort by type then link, rewrite the table, and save. Additions will be curated manually from existing notes. An alternative default text is also needed.

An implement default text is being added. Multiple extends rows will be kept and sorted together. Index slugs are defined as getting-started, eternal-principles, and maps-of-content. Meta pages will be left untouched. The getting-started note will drop its index row but retain its extends links and gain a contradicts link to Maps of Content.

The ADDITIONS map will be built as a slug-to-list dictionary, converting titles into wiki-links. Minimal alternative relationships will be added for gospel notes where sibling practices compete. Gospel implementations will be skipped. A subagent was considered but rejected; the script will be written directly with embedded logic.

The script will auto-infer alternative relationships from symmetric contradicts pairs already recorded in the garden. When note A contradicts B and B also contradicts A, the script adds an alternative row from A to B. This avoids manually curating every pair.

The ADDITIONS dictionary will cover roughly fifty-five PKM notes, with manual curation for all entries. A separate AUTO_ALTERNATIVE_PAIRS list will handle symmetric alternative relationships. Implementations are being mapped for specific workflows such as weekly-review-checklists, the-second-brain-workflow, active-knowledge-curation, capture, and minimum-viable-product.

Mapping additional implementations: the-feynman-technique to Evergreen Notes, evergreen-notes to Atomic Notes, and periodic-knowledge-review to Active Knowledge Curation. Skipping the GTD versus PARA comparison as a direct implementation. Starting a symmetric alternative tool pair list for capture methods.

Three more symmetric alternative tool pairs were added to the curated list. PARA Method and associative-linking contrast folder sorting against link-first organization. Local-first software and building-a-personal-api weigh plain files against structured APIs. Progressive summarization and the Feynman technique compare layered explanations against bold layers.

Four more symmetric alternative tool pairs were added. Spaced repetition and the Feynman technique contrast flashcards against explaining gaps. RSS for research and signal versus noise weigh standing feeds against inbox filtering. Digital garden and drafting in public compare slow public pacing against shipping drafts for feedback. Mind mapping and atomic notes contrast one bubble map against one claim per file.

Three more symmetric alternative tool pairs were added. Daily notes and evergreen notes contrast scratch pads against durable phrasing. Metadata strategy and building a personal API weigh light tags against machine-readable schemas. The garage concept and digital garden contrast private drafts against public visibility.</details></think>

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

Writing and running the sync script with curated additions for all garden notes.

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd /Users/jonathanrapusas/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && hugo --gc --minify 2>&1 | tail -8 && python3 << 'PY'
import re, json
from pathlib import Path
NOTES = Path("content/english/notes")
TYPE_ORDER = {"alternative": 0, "contradicts": 1, "extends": 2, "implements": 3}
row_re = re.compile(r"^\|\s*(alternative|contradicts|extends|implements|index)\s*\|")
stats = {"index_rows": 0, "unsorted": 0, "index_kind": 0, "note_tables": 0}
for f in NOTES.glob("*.md"):
    t = f.read_text()
    if 'note_kind: "index"' in t: stats["index_kind"] += 1
    if 'note_kind: "meta"' in t: continue
    if "## Note Relationships" not in t: continue
    stats["note_tables"] += 1
    body = t.split("---", 2)[-1]
    sec = body.split("## Note Relationships", 1)[1].split("\n## ", 1)[0]
    types = []
    for line in sec.splitlines():
        m = row_re.match(line.strip())
        if m:
            if m.group(1) == "index": stats["index_rows"] += 1
            types.append(TYPE_ORDER.get(m.group(1), 99))
    if types != sorted(types):
        stats["unsorted"] += 1
        print("unsorted:", f.stem, types)
print(json.dumps(stats, indent=2))
PY</summary>

```bash
cd /Users/jonathanrapusas/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && hugo --gc --minify 2>&1 | tail -8 && python3 << 'PY'
import re, json
from pathlib import Path
NOTES = Path("content/english/notes")
TYPE_ORDER = {"alternative": 0, "contradicts": 1, "extends": 2, "implements": 3}
row_re = re.compile(r"^\|\s*(alternative|contradicts|extends|implements|index)\s*\|")
stats = {"index_rows": 0, "unsorted": 0, "index_kind": 0, "note_tables": 0}
for f in NOTES.glob("*.md"):
    t = f.read_text()
    if 'note_kind: "index"' in t: stats["index_kind"] += 1
    if 'note_kind: "meta"' in t: continue
    if "## Note Relationships" not in t: continue
    stats["note_tables"] += 1
    body = t.split("---", 2)[-1]
    sec = body.split("## Note Relationships", 1)[1].split("\n## ", 1)[0]
    types = []
    for line in sec.splitlines():
        m = row_re.match(line.strip())
        if m:
            if m.group(1) == "index": stats["index_rows"] += 1
            types.append(TYPE_ORDER.get(m.group(1), 99))
    if types != sorted(types):
        stats["unsorted"] += 1
        print("unsorted:", f.stem, types)
print(json.dumps(stats, indent=2))
PY
```

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

Done. Here's what changed:

### `index` → frontmatter flag
Three hub pages now use `note_kind: index`:
- [[Getting Started]]
- [[Eternal Principles]]
- [[Maps of Content]]

New partial `is-index-page.html` drives graph **Index** filter and list `data-note-rel-index`. The `| index |` row type is removed from `relationship-types.html`.

### Four pair types only (sorted)
Every atomic note table is sorted: **alternative → contradicts → extends → implements**.

87 garden notes updated. PKM notes got **implements** and **alternative** where they fit (e.g. [[Analog Capture Tools]] now has all four). Gospel notes got alternatives only where sibling practices genuinely compete (e.g. [[Secret Devotion]] ↔ [[Let Your Light Shine]]) — not forced filler on every faith note.

~50 **future-note** `extends` stubs kept or added ([[Zettelkasten]], [[Sermon on the Mount]], [[Frictionless Capture]], etc.) as broken links for Issues.

### Page kinds (documented in [[Getting Started]])

| `note_kind` | Role | Table required? |
|-------------|------|-----------------|
| `note` (default) | Atomic claim | Yes — extends + contradicts minimum |
| `index` | Hub / MOC | No — optional |
| `meta` | Utility tool | No — URL only, no wikilinks |

### Files touched
- Templates: `relationship-types.html`, `collect-note-issues.html`, `graph-data.html`, `list.html`, new `is-index-page.html`
- Docs: `note-relationships.md` (4 types + updated flashcard for hubs)
- All 87 content notes + `scripts/sync-note-relationships.py` for future normalization

Hugo build passes. Zero `| index |` rows remain; all tables sorted.

<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd /Users/jonathanrapusas/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && python3 << 'PY'
import re
from pathlib import Path
from collections import Counter
c = Counter()
by_kind = {"note": Counter(), "index": Counter(), "meta": Counter()}
for f in Path("content/english/notes").glob("*.md"):
    if f.name.startswith("_"): continue
    t = f.read_text()
    kind = "meta" if 'note_kind: "meta"' in t else "index" if 'note_kind: "index"' in t else "note"
    for m in re.finditer(r'^## (.+)$', t, re.M):
        h = m.group(1)
        c[h] += 1
        by_kind[kind][h] += 1
print("=== ALL HEADINGS (count) ===")
for h, n in c.most_common():
    print(f"{n:3}  {h}")
print("\n=== INDEX pages only ===")
for h, n in by_kind["index"].most_common():
    print(f"{n:3}  {h}")
print("\n=== META pages only ===")
for h, n in by_kind["meta"].most_common():
    print(f"{n:3}  {h}")
PY</summary>

```bash
cd /Users/jonathanrapusas/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && python3 << 'PY'
import re
from pathlib import Path
from collections import Counter
c = Counter()
by_kind = {"note": Counter(), "index": Counter(), "meta": Counter()}
for f in Path("content/english/notes").glob("*.md"):
    if f.name.startswith("_"): continue
    t = f.read_text()
    kind = "meta" if 'note_kind: "meta"' in t else "index" if 'note_kind: "index"' in t else "note"
    for m in re.finditer(r'^## (.+)$', t, re.M):
        h = m.group(1)
        c[h] += 1
        by_kind[kind][h] += 1
print("=== ALL HEADINGS (count) ===")
for h, n in c.most_common():
    print(f"{n:3}  {h}")
print("\n=== INDEX pages only ===")
for h, n in by_kind["index"].most_common():
    print(f"{n:3}  {h}")
print("\n=== META pages only ===")
for h, n in by_kind["meta"].most_common():
    print(f"{n:3}  {h}")
PY
```

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Checking weekly-review-checklists, free-grace, and building-a-second-brain for extra sections. Comparing single.html rendering against markdown.</details></think>

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

Sections split into three buckets: what you write in markdown, what the theme injects at render time, and what's allowed by `note_kind`.

## By page kind

### `note_kind: note` (default — atomic garden notes)

**Standard body sections (what almost every note has):**

| Section | Required? | Purpose |
|---------|-----------|---------|
| **Opening prose** | Yes | One-breath definition + claim (no `##` needed) |
| **## Note Relationships** | Yes (linted) | Table: alternative → contradicts → extends → implements |
| **## See also** | Optional in markdown | Legacy/editor hints; **stripped at render** — the theme builds See also from tags |

That's the atomic pattern from [[Getting Started]] and [[Atomic Notes]]: *"No extra `##` sections beyond **Note Relationships**"* — one claim in prose, typed links in the table, everything else via wikilinks and hubs.

**Extra `##` sections are discouraged** on atomic notes. If you need a second heading, you probably need a second note. The old "non-atomic heading" linter was removed for this reason.

**Rare exceptions in the garden today:**
- [[Mental Models List]] has **## Models** (list hub inside one note — borderline MOC, not atomic)

---

### `note_kind: index` (hub / MOC — 3 pages)

These **can** have many `##` sections because they gather links, not one claim.

| Page | Extra sections |
|------|----------------|
| **[[Getting Started]]** | Atomic rule, Create a note, Link between notes, Page kinds, Utility surfaces, Explore, Publish to the blog |
| **[[Maps of Content]]** | **## Maps** (+ `###` subgroups: Foundations, Capture, …) |
| **[[Eternal Principles]]** | Theological basis, The two great commandments, How to treat others, Heart and priorities, Character and witness, Church and world, Cross-links (PKM) |

Optional **## Note Relationships** table (not required for index pages).

---

### `note_kind: meta` (utility — Graph, Issues, Review, etc.)

No **## Note Relationships** table. Body is tool docs only.

Example: [[Flashcards]] has **## Frontmatter format (review notes)**.

Link from content notes by **URL**, not `[[wikilink]]`.

---

## Rendered on the note page (not in your markdown)

These appear on `/notes/<slug>/` from templates, regardless of what you write:

| Block | Source |
|-------|--------|
| Toolbar | Layout |
| Title (`h1`) | Frontmatter `title` |
| Status + dates | Frontmatter `status` + file dates |
| Tag chips | Frontmatter `tags` |
| **Link issues** | Linter (broken links, unlinked mentions, missing extends/contradicts) |
| **Table of contents** | Auto from body `##` headings (sidebar on desktop) |
| **Flashcards** | Frontmatter `cards:` when `review: true` |
| **Links from this Note** | Outgoing wikilinks in body |
| **See also** | Tag-related notes (dynamic — not your markdown `## See also`) |
| **Backlinks** | Inbound wikilinks |

---

## Quick decision guide

```
Atomic claim?     → prose + ## Note Relationships only
Gathering many?   → note_kind: index + topic ## sections + wikilink lists  
Tool/docs page?   → note_kind: meta + whatever ## sections help; URL links only
Flashcards?       → frontmatter cards: (renders Flashcards block, not a ## section)
Related notes?    → don't write ## See also — tags + theme handle it
```

**Rule of thumb:** For a normal note, the only structural section you should plan for is **## Note Relationships**. Everything else is either prose, a hub/index exception, or injected by the theme.

---

_**User**_

what sections could be added?

---

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
The user wants suggestions for additional note sections in their garden.</details></think>

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

Sections worth adding depend on **page kind**. Most new sections belong on **index/meta** pages or as **theme blocks**, not as extra `##` headings on atomic notes.

## Atomic notes (`note_kind: note`) — add sparingly

The garden rule stays: **prose + ## Note Relationships**. Extra headings usually mean the note should split.

If you really need one more body section, these are the only ones that fit the atomic model:

| Section | When it helps | Caveat |
|---------|---------------|--------|
| **## Example** | One concrete scenario that makes the claim stick | Keep to one example; second idea → new note |
| **## When I use this** | Habit trigger — "I reach for this when…" | Good for PKM workflow notes; gospel notes rarely need it |
| **## Source** | One citation (book, verse block, talk) | Prefer [[Literature Notes]] as a separate note if the source is big |

Everything else (related ideas, tensions, tools) already lives in **Note Relationships** and wikilinks. I'd avoid adding **Summary**, **Bottom line**, or **Pairs with** — those were old patterns you're moving away from.

---

## Index pages (`note_kind: index`) — most room to grow

Hubs are where extra sections make sense. Useful additions:

| Section | Purpose |
|---------|---------|
| **## Start here** | 3–5 entry notes for newcomers (Getting Started already does this informally) |
| **## By topic** / **## Maps** | What MOCs already use — curated wikilink lists under `###` groups |
| **## Orphans / gaps** | Notes you know you still need to write (broken links on purpose) |
| **## Recently updated** | Could be theme-driven from file dates — no manual section needed |
| **## Cross-links** | Eternal Principles' **Cross-links (PKM)** — faith ↔ productivity bridges |

Eternal Principles could also use **## Drill** (link to Review + which `card_sets` match each section) — right now that's in prose.

---

## Meta pages (`note_kind: meta`) — docs sections

Utility pages can have whatever `##` sections explain the tool:

| Section | Example use |
|---------|-------------|
| **## How to use** | Graph, Path, Queue |
| **## Filters / shortcuts** | Graph rank filters, list status filters |
| **## What gets excluded** | What's not in the graph/random pool |
| **## Lint rules** | Issues page (partially there already) |

---

## Theme blocks (best bang for buck)

These add "sections" **without** breaking the atomic rule — rendered from frontmatter or graph data, not extra `##` in markdown:

| Block | Data source | Value |
|-------|-------------|-------|
| **Related by relationship type** | Note Relationships table | "Also extends [[X]]" across the garden |
| **Contradicts cluster** | Typed links | Show tension neighbors on the note page |
| **Backlink snippet** | Already exists | You have this |
| **Cards** | `review: true` + `cards:` | You have this |
| **Stale / status callout** | `status` + file age | "This note hasn't been touched in 90 days" |
| **Open issues** | Issues linter | You have this on-page |
| **Path to / from hub** | Graph | "2 hops from [[PKM]]" on note pages |

These keep atomic bodies clean while making the **single note view** richer.

---

## Frontmatter "sections" (not markdown)

Sometimes better than `##` headings:

| Field | Acts like |
|-------|-----------|
| `card_sets:` | Groups flashcards (already used) |
| `aliases:` | Alternate link targets (already used) |
| `status:` | seedling / evergreen / inbox / archived |
| `note_kind:` | index / meta / note |
| Future: `hub:` or `parent:` | Which MOC owns this note (optional — backlinks + MOC lists may be enough) |

---

## What I'd **not** add

- **## Tags** / **## Categories** — already in frontmatter chips
- **## See also** in markdown — theme generates it; static lists rot
- **## Backlinks** in markdown — theme generates it
- **## Flashcards** in body — use frontmatter `cards:`
- **## Pipeline** (capture → distill → express) — keep as prose in [[Building a Second Brain]], not a sixth relationship type or repeated section
- **Per-note ## Contradicts / ## Extends** — that's what the table replaced

---

## Practical recommendation

**If you're choosing one thing to add:**

1. **Theme blocks** first (relationship neighbors, stale badge) — zero markdown discipline cost  
2. **Index-only sections** second (`## Start here`, `## Gaps`) — only on the 3 hub pages  
3. **Atomic body sections** last — only `## Example` or `## Source` when one note truly needs it  

The garden stays healthiest when atomic notes stay at **two blocks**: opening claim + **Note Relationships**. Hubs and the theme carry everything else.

If you want to go further, we could pick one — e.g. a **Start here** block on index pages, or a **Contradicts neighbors** panel on single notes — and wire it up.

<think><details><summary>Thought Process</summary>
Evaluating each proposed section against the system.

Practical Examples could be a single example section or woven into the opening prose. Flashcards already provide scenario-based examples.

The "Why Is This Important" section risks redundancy and essay bloat. Atomic notes already open with why the claim matters.

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

These four map cleanly onto what you already have — but **not as four standard `##` sections on every atomic note**. Here's where each belongs.

---

### Practical Examples

**Fit:** High value, but **not as a fixed heading on every note**.

| Approach | When |
|----------|------|
| **One line in opening prose** | Default — "Paper wins in sermons when the phone dies" |
| **## Example** (single, optional) | When the claim needs one concrete scene to land |
| **Second note + wikilink** | Second example = different claim → split |
| **Flashcard front** | Spine notes already — scenario *is* the practical example |

Your cards skill literally trains **life's cue → act now**. That's "practical example" without a section. Adding **## Practical Examples** on 87 notes would duplicate flashcards on spine notes and bloat atomic notes elsewhere.

**Recommendation:** Optional **## Example** at most (one per note). Otherwise prose or a linked note like [[Minimum Viable Product]] → concrete ship story as its own atom.

---

### Why Is This Important

**Fit:** Usually **already covered** — redundant as its own section.

You already have:
- Opening **one-breath definition** (the "why" in plain language)
- **Reason** column on **extends** rows ("what this builds on")
- **contradicts** with **when** (why the tradeoff matters)

A **## Why Is This Important** section tends to become a second essay — the thing [[Atomic Notes]] pushes against ("mini blog post with multiple sections").

**Recommendation:** Skip as a section. If "why" isn't clear, fix the **opening sentence** or the **extends Reason**, not add a heading.

---

### How to explain this to an X year old

**Fit:** Strong idea, **wrong default shape** as a body section.

This *is* [[The Feynman Technique]] — teach-it-simple to expose gaps. Good content, but:

- Not every note needs it (garden meta, pure workflow labels)
- Age varies by idea (gospel ethics → ~10–12; PKM abstractions → ~14–16; grace vs works → ~8 with a story)
- A full **## Explain to a 12-year-old** on every page fights atomic structure

Better homes:

| Home | Use |
|------|-----|
| **One sentence in prose** | "I'd tell a kid: …" when it clarifies the claim |
| **Flashcard back** | Spine notes — simple apply line *is* the kid version |
| **Optional frontmatter** | e.g. `explain_age: 12` + `explain: "..."` if you want it structured without `##` |
| **Dedicated note** | Feynman technique already covers the *method*; each atom doesn't repeat the method |

**Age guide (if you use it at all):**

| Age band | Good for |
|----------|----------|
| **6–8** | Concrete actions — share, forgive, tell the truth |
| **10–12** | Beatitudes, neighbor love, simple PKM habits |
| **14–16** | Tradeoffs — PARA vs links, signal vs noise |
| **Adult** | Skip kid frame — use normal prose |

**Recommendation:** Optional **one line** in prose or a **card back** on review notes. Not a standard `##` block. If you formalize it, use frontmatter on spine notes only, not 87 headings.

---

### Source

**Fit:** **Yes — but usually as a link, not a section**.

| Type | Pattern |
|------|---------|
| **Book / talk / article** | `extends` → [[Literature Notes]] or future [[Author Name]] stub; or one **## Source** line with title + optional URL |
| **Scripture** | Verse in prose or `extends` → [[Matthew 5]] (you already do this on gospel notes) |
| **Your own experience** | Prose — no section needed |
| **Obvious lineage** | **extends** row + Reason (Zettelkasten, Forte, etc.) |

[[Intellectual Sourcing]] is the home note for "where did this come from." Most atoms don't need **## Source** unless citation matters for trust or you'd cite it in conversation.

**Recommendation:** 
- Default: **extends** wikilink to source note (broken link OK for future literature note)
- When you need attribution on-page: optional **## Source** — one bullet, not a bibliography

---

## Summary: add as standard sections?

| Proposed section | Standard on every atomic note? | Better home |
|------------------|-------------------------------|-------------|
| Practical Examples | No | Prose, optional ## Example, flashcard front, split note |
| Why Is This Important | No | Opening line + Reason column |
| Explain to X year old | No | Prose one-liner, card back, or frontmatter on spine notes |
| Source | Sometimes | extends → literature/scripture note; optional ## Source |

**If you formalize anything**, I'd pick at most two optional atomic sections:

```
## Example          (one scene, optional)
## Source           (one citation, optional)
```

…and keep **Why** in prose + table, **Explain simple** on Feynman/spine flashcards — not four new headings on every note.

Want these two optional sections documented in [[Getting Started]] / [[Atomic Notes]], or a frontmatter schema (`example:`, `source:`, `explain_age:`) so the theme can render them without extra `##` blocks?

---

_**User**_

Add Example

import re, pathlib
allowed = {"Note Relationships", "See also", "Example"}
for p in sorted(pathlib.Path("content/english/notes").glob("*.md")):
    text = p.read_text()
    if 'note_kind: "index"' in text or 'note_kind: index' in text:
        continue
    if 'note_kind: "meta"' in text or 'note_kind: meta' in text:
        continue
    if p.name in ('_index.md',):
        continue
    extras = []
    for m in re.finditer(r'^## (.+)$', text, re.M):
        h = m.group(1).strip()
        if h not in allowed:
            extras.append(h)
    if extras:
        print(f"{p.name}: {extras}")

_**Agent (auto-low)**_

`## Example` is now a first-class optional section on atomic notes.

### What changed

**Docs**
- [[Getting Started]] — atomic rule allows optional **## Example** before **## Note Relationships**
- [[Atomic Notes]] — includes a live **## Example** (split vs. kitchen-sink)
- [[Note Relationships]] — body shape: prose → optional Example → relationships table

**Lint** (`collect-note-issues.html`)
- Atomic notes may only use: `## Example`, `## Note Relationships`, `## See also`
- Anything else → **Extra section** on `/notes/issues/` and the note’s issue panel

**Fix**
- [[Mental Models List]] → `note_kind: index` (hub with `## Models`; not an atomic note)

### Shape for atomic notes

```markdown
One-breath claim in prose.

## Example          ← optional, one scene max

## Note Relationships   ← required

## See also         ← optional in markdown (stripped at render)
```

Second example → new note and wikilink. I can add `## Example` to specific notes if you name which ones.

---

_**User**_

review all notes to provide examples

---

<tool-use data-tool-type="shell" data-tool-name="run_terminal_command_v2">
<details><summary>Tool use: **run_terminal_command_v2** • Run command: cd /Users/jonathanrapusas/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && python3 - <<'PY'
import re, pathlib
notes = []
for p in sorted(pathlib.Path("content/english/notes").glob("*.md")):
    if p.name == "_index.md":
        continue
    text = p.read_text()
    kind = "note"
    m = re.search(r'^note_kind:\s*"?(\w+)"?', text, re.M)
    if m:
        kind = m.group(1)
    has_example = "## Example" in text
    title_m = re.search(r'^title:\s*"([^"]+)"', text, re.M)
    title = title_m.group(1) if title_m else p.stem
    notes.append((kind, has_example, title, p.name))
print(f"Total: {len(notes)}")
for kind in ("note", "index", "meta"):
    subset = [n for n in notes if n[0]==kind]
    ex = sum(1 for n in subset if n[1])
    print(f"  {kind}: {len(subset)} ({ex} with Example)")
print("\nAtomic without Example:")
for kind, has_ex, title, name in notes:
    if kind == "note" and not has_ex:
        print(name)
PY</summary>

```bash
cd /Users/jonathanrapusas/Documents/GitHub/jorap.com && python3 - <<'PY'
import re, pathlib
notes = []
for p in sorted(pathlib.Path("content/english/notes").glob("*.md")):
    if p.name == "_index.md":
        continue
    text = p.read_text()
    kind = "note"
    m = re.search(r'^note_kind:\s*"?(\w+)"?', text, re.M)
    if m:
        kind = m.group(1)
    has_example = "## Example" in text
    title_m = re.search(r'^title:\s*"([^"]+)"', text, re.M)
    title = title_m.group(1) if title_m else p.stem
    notes.append((kind, has_example, title, p.name))
print(f"Total: {len(notes)}")
for kind in ("note", "index", "meta"):
    subset = [n for n in notes if n[0]==kind]
    ex = sum(1 for n in subset if n[1])
    print(f"  {kind}: {len(subset)} ({ex} with Example)")
print("\nAtomic without Example:")
for kind, has_ex, title, name in notes:
    if kind == "note" and not has_ex:
        print(name)
PY
```
